summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host and wallet data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three reachable modules — build-verifier.js, scanner.js, and wallet-scanner.js — that collectively enumerate the installer's host (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, cwd, whoami), invoke child_process for shell reconnaissance (execSync, curl, ping, docker push), and POST the collected data over HTTPS to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js lines 161 and 323). The wallet-scanner.js module additionally performs ~1300 lines of network-command + child_process activity targeting wallet-related paths and exfiltrates via POST to the same C2 family. The package name suggests a benign Solidity deployment safety helper, but the shipped code is a credential/host/wallet stealer with a hardcoded GitHub Pages drop-site and outbound POSTs from multiple modules. Two unambiguous attack fingerprints are present: hardcoded attacker C2 in reachable code, and host/wallet enumeration with outbound HTTPS exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
